sentence: Subjective:

HPI: Stephanie Morales, a 44-year-old female, presents today 6 weeks status post right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty, reports no problems with the knee, but mentions numbness on the side of the knee that will improve as the nerves heal.
The patient is happy with the surgery and has no pain.

Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's gait was antalgic with a slight limp and decreased velocity, not using an assistive device.
The right knee exam showed a healed surgical scar, mild swelling, tenderness along the area of the incision, and range of motion of 0 to 120 degrees, with everything else normal.
Test Results: Radiographs of the right knee, taken today, show implants in a stable position with no evidence of loosening or early complications.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is 6 weeks postoperative with a stable and healing unicompartmental arthroplasty, with some expected numbness on the side of the knee that will improve with time.
Problem list: [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed no new medications.
I instructed the patient to continue with physical therapy to work on strength, range of motion, and endurance.
I also told the patient that it's common to experience aches and pains occasionally, but they should improve over the next 2-3 months.
I will write a note to excuse the patient from work for another 6 weeks, limiting lifting to less than 5-10 pounds and avoiding standing or sitting for more than 30 minutes.
I will discuss the patient's job further in 6 weeks.
The patient is cleared to drive when they feel safe and ready.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a telemedicine visit in 6 weeks to check on progress and make sure everything is okay.
